verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is a tetrameric IP3-gated Ca2+ release channel canonically resident in the ER membrane (endomembrane). However, a documented plasma membrane pool of IP3R3 has been reported in multiple cell types (e.g., polarized epithelial cells, B cells, and at ER-PM junctions), where it can contribute to Ca2+ entry/flux across the PM with the large cytosolic ligand-binding/regulatory domain facing the cytosol and the channel pore traversing the membrane. Given documented minority PM localization alongside its dominant ER residence, this fits dual_localization rather than pure endomembrane_resident. Topology means any extracellular-facing loops are small, so accessibility is limited.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether the reported PM pool of ITPR3 is robust and generalizable enough, and whether any extracellular loop is substantial enough to be a credible binder epitope.
